page: Microsoft Azure F64amds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Famdsv7 family. It features 64 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The CPU configuration includes a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

This server is configured with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local data storage, the instance includes 7559 GB of local disk capacity and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this server configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the F64amds_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple domains. Both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests place in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory subsystem performance is also highly rated, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 11893.77 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 193.26 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations similarly achieve top-tier status, with Redis handling 170.17 million operations per second.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage capacity, this server offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and data-heavy workloads. The combination of dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high memory bandwidth makes it suitable for large-scale database hosting, memory-intensive analytics, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure F64amds_v7 is a memory-intensive server from the Famdsv7 family, powered by a 64-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor on the x86_64 architecture. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 64 dedicated physical cores, 512.0 GB of RAM, and 7559 GB of local storage, with no integrated GPU. The hardware supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions, including AVX-512 and VAES. In benchmark testing, the F64amds_v7 delivers top-tier performance in CPU processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET throughput reaching 170.17 million operations per second. Its high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core and substantial local disk capacity make it qualitatively cost-efficient for resource-dense deployments. This server is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F64amds_v7 is a memory-intensive server featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 cores, 512 GB of RAM, and 7559 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier database and compute performance.

tagline: A high-capacity, memory-intensive server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and massive local storage for demanding database and analytical workloads.

512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
7559 GB of local storage capacity with Premium Storage capability
